title: Personal
text: Outside the lab and the terminal, I spend time with clay and a camera. A small selection of work follows. All photographs are mine; ceramics are made and fired by me unless noted.

title: Personal

title: Personal
text:
Photography






Madonna di Campiglio, Dolomites · 2026







Reading riverside, late autumn · 2024









East Tyrol approach, dusk · 2025







Studio, working light · 2025











NoteNotes on the work





I’m interested in the same thing across both media: surfaces that record process. With clay, that’s tool marks, finger pulls, the way a glaze breaks over an edge. With photography, it’s light catching texture — frost, fabric, weathered timber.
Both also reward patience in a way the rest of my work doesn’t.

title: Projects & Decks
text:
Slide decks
Drop .pdf (preferred) or .pptx files into the decks/ directory in the repo and link them here. PDFs render cleanly in a browser; PowerPoints download.

Example: Platelet modelling — work in progress
Example: wcEcoli architecture walkthrough







TipTip



For PowerPoint, export to PDF before committing. PPTX files inflate repo size quickly and don’t preview in a browser. Or write future decks in revealjs directly in Quarto.

title: Setting up this site
section: What Quarto gives you out of the box
text:
What Quarto gives you out of the box
Code blocks with syntax highlighting:
import numpy as np

def hill(x, k, n):
    """Hill function for cooperative binding."""
    return x**n / (k**n + x**n)
Inline maths: \(K_d = \frac{[A][B]}{[AB]}\), and display maths:
\[
\frac{d[Ca^{2+}]_i}{dt} = J_{IP_3R} + J_{leak} - J_{SERCA}
\]

Note



You can use BibTeX citations with [@key] syntax — Quarto reads a references.bib in the project root.

title: Steve Haigh
text:
Software developer of 30 years, now researching biomedical sciences at the University of Reading. My dissertation extends the wcEcoli whole-cell modelling framework to build the first computational model of a human platelet, supervised by Dr Mike Fry.
This site collects notes, slide decks, and project write-ups from the intersection of software and wet-lab biology.

title: Steve Haigh
text:
Currently working on

Whole-cell modelling of human platelet activation
Containerised simulation pipelines and Dash-based exploration UIs
Bridging classical software engineering practice with computational biology workflows

title: Steve Haigh
text:
Elsewhere
See the Projects page for repos and decks, or the Blog for longer-form notes.

title: About
text: Thirty years building software, currently retraining as a research scientist. My M.Res at the University of Reading focuses on the feasibility of constructing the first whole-cell computational model of a human platelet by extending the wcEcoli framework (Karr et al. 2012; Macklin et al. 2020).

title: About
text: Thirty years building software, currently retraining as a research scientist. My M.Res at the University of Reading focuses on the feasibility of constructing the first whole-cell computational model of a human platelet by extending the wcEcoli framework (Karr et al. 2012; Macklin et al. 2020).

title: About
text:
Research interests

Whole-cell modelling and rule-based systems biology
Platelet activation, calcium signalling, and granule dynamics
Reproducible computational pipelines for wet-lab integration
Practical applications of LLMs to scientific software engineering
